summary: Completed end-to-end backend + frontend testing for Cultivate. All 35 pytest backend tests pass (100%) covering config/field/circles/meetings/auth/RSVP/organizer/calendar/resources/notifications/payments. All critical frontend UX flows verified: spatial field navigation (location -> circle -> panel), deep links (/place/:label, /circle/:slug), email+password auth (register+login+logout), RSVP request flow end-to-end (member sees pending; organizer sees the request; approve -> member sees exact_location + CONNECTED status), organizer schedule-form toggle, protected route redirect to /login, directory tag filter + suggest form. No blocking issues found.

issues: Circle-node labels overlap slightly at 1920px (Riverbed Circle / High Desert Walkers labels touching TEST circle created by tests). Non-blocking, purely aesthetic; will clean up once test circles are removed.

action_items: Optional: Suppress harmless React Router future-flag warnings in console.
Optional: Guest-side /auth/me returns 401 which is expected but shows as console error; could be swallowed in AuthContext for cleaner console.
Optional cleanup: pytest creates a `TEST Integration Hub ...` circle each run which persists; the test could delete it or use a delete endpoint (none exists currently).
critical_code_review_comments: seed() is guarded by `db.circles.count_documents({}) > 0` so it is idempotent, but note the seeded meetings use relative-day offsets from `now_utc()` at first-run only; if the DB is not wiped, upcoming meetings could drift into the past over time (already visible for High Desert Walkers/Riverbed which now show July dates). Consider a periodic re-seed or a date-relative computed view.
server.py combines all domains (~560 lines) in a single file; refactor into routers per domain (auth, circles, meetings, rsvps, resources, notifications) for maintainability if it grows.
POST /api/circles allows any authenticated user to become an organizer with zero validation (no name uniqueness beyond slug, no rate-limit). Fine for MVP; flag for future abuse control.

In-app notifications for RSVP status use the same `rsvp_status` type for both approved/declined; consumers cannot distinguish without parsing the title. Consider a separate `status` field in the notification meta.
get_current_user does not extend session expiry on activity; sessions expire strictly 7 days after creation. Acceptable for MVP.

CORS is `allow_origin_regex='.*'` with `allow_credentials=True` which browsers actually reject for wildcard + credentials. In the preview environment this works only because the frontend and backend share the same host. Should be pinned to the frontend origin for production.
test_credentials: organizer@cultivatesf.org / member@cultivatesf.org / admin@cultivatesf.org — all password Cultivate123!
seed_data_creation: Backend seed.py auto-seeds 3 users, 6 circles, meetings, and resources on first startup. Tests create a persistent 'TEST Integration Hub <id>' circle per run (no delete endpoint available).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is a full regression harness (35 tests, ~6s). Uses Bearer tokens (session_tokens returned by /api/auth/login). The RSVP flow already ran against `riverbed-circle` for member@cultivatesf.org so on re-run the RSVP status will already be approved (tests are idempotent). Frontend spatial field is async — always wait ~2s after clicking a location/circle node. Deep links /place/:label and /circle/:slug are supported. Email delivery is intentionally MOCKED (logged only via services.py). Verify notifications via GET /api/notifications, not email.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues — nothing to RCA.
